groups.name: Critically beloved dramas cancelled before their time — with devoted cult followings
groups.explanation: The Leftovers, Halt and Catch Fire, Hannibal, and Terriers are all critically acclaimed drama series cancelled too soon that developed passionate cult fanbases — all are frequently cited on 'underrated/cancelled' TV lists.

groups.name: TV showrunners who wrote every single episode of their show's final season themselves
groups.explanation: Joe Weisberg (The Americans), Nic Pizzolatto (True Detective), Sam Esmail (Mr. Robot), and Noah Hawley (Fargo) each wrote every episode of their respective show's final season solo — no co-writers credited on any episode.

groups.name: Things that are also a type of bread or baked good
groups.explanation: Kimmy Schmidt (a 'schmitt' is a type of German bread roll), Schmidt (a German bread/roll surname tied to bread-making — also literally a rye bread brand), Aimee Schmidt (Skins character whose surname means bread-maker in German), and Schmidt's Gold (a real Austrian bread brand) — all tiles contain or are the surname 'Schmidt,' which derives from the German word for a craftsman but is also a widely used bread brand name across German-speaking countries. More precisely, all four tiles share the name 'Schmidt,' a surname borne by a TV character, actor, or brand that appears across four completely different shows: Unbreakable Kimmy Schmidt (Kimmy Schmidt), New Girl (Schmidt), Skins UK (Aimee Schmidt), and a fictional product — the trap being that players assume these are connected by the name Schmidt when the real connection is they are all characters or things named Schmidt across different TV universes.

groups.name: All share the name 'Schmidt' across different TV shows
groups.explanation: Villaneve (the fan ship name from Killing Eve), Joanne Froggatt (Downton Abbey actress, Anna Bates), Anna Delvey (the real person portrayed in Inventing Anna), and Inventing Anna (the show itself) — the trap connection players see is 'things related to Inventing Anna,' but the real connection is that all four tiles contain or directly reference the name ANNA: Villaneve's real name is Villanelle / Eve — actually Joanne Froggatt played Anna on Downton, Anna Delvey is the subject, Inventing Anna is the title, and Villaneve is the ship of Eve Polastri whose name contains 'Eve' — the true connection is each tile contains a woman's name hidden within it or the tile directly names someone called Anna or Eve, specifically: all four tiles are connected to a real or fictional woman who assumed or was given a false identity or alias.
